zotero.abstractNote: We consider the problem of computing the integral of a Gaussian probability density function over a domain defined by a set of linear inequality constraints. This problem is equivalent to computing the cumulative distribution function of a multivariate Gaussian distribution. While this is a well-studied problem, existing methods often struggle with high-dimensional domains or require expensive numerical integration. We propose a new approach based on the observation that the integral can be expressed as a sequence of one-dimensional integrals. We show that this approach is computationally efficient and provides accurate results even in high dimensions. We demonstrate the effectiveness of our method on a range of synthetic and real-world problems.
zotero.conferenceName: 23rd International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Statistics
zotero.proceedingsTitle: Proceedings of the 23rd International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Statistics
